description: The declared release-gate thresholds for ADR-051 §7. Split into (1) ACTIVE local-regression tolerances that gate day-to-day changes against the committed provisional baseline (scripts/adr051_slo_gate.py), (2) the production SLOs whose absolute numbers are measured on the declared hardware class via the GKE fleet run and enforced by scripts/slo_gate.py, and (3) the ALWAYS-ON absolute correctness gates that are hardware-independent.

local_regression.rationale: Hardware-independent: catches a local change that regresses the serving path against its own recorded baseline, without pretending a dev box states production capacity. Enforced by scripts/adr051_slo_gate.py against a fresh run of the baseline anchor config.

tolerances.comment: A fresh anchor run must sustain >= 85% of baseline throughput and stay within the stated multiples of baseline added-latency percentiles. Bands are wider on the tail (p999) than the median because the tail is noisier on a co-located box; tighten them once a dedicated loadgen removes that noise.

per_core_scaling.comment: NOT gated locally: the 1->N curve is non-authoritative on a single box (co-located loadgen contends for cores). Recorded only; the authoritative curve lives in production_slo below.

production_slo.measured_on: 2026-07-14; GKE us-central1-a STANDARD zonal cluster mcp-re-fleet (project MCP-S tests), branch mcpre-122 delegated-required-only serving path (RFC 9421 + RFC 9530), proxy image 0.12.1-wifix (fixes the WI metadata token URL). ROOT ISSUER in Cloud KMS via WORKLOAD IDENTITY (metadata token bound to mcp-re-kms-signer with cloudkms.signerVerifier; no key material in-pod, no user token). tls_load_harness_bench built --features async_serve,redis_replay, run as a K8s Job with in-pod primary+2-replica Redis native-sidecar (durability tier redis-wait-quorum:2:2000); cold TLS1.3-mTLS, concurrency 128, 8000 requests/run (v2 canonical envelope); MCP_RE_LOADGEN_CORES 1 then 8; in-process HTTP echo inner. Enforced by scripts/slo_gate.py; both classes gated PASS.
production_slo.hardware_note: The DECLARED floor is the WEAKER of the two measured classes (e2-standard-8, 402.1 rps 8-core); a run on that class or better clears these targets (c3-standard-8, 499.4 rps, also clears). Throughput floor ~=0.62x the weaker measured 8-core median; latency ceilings ~=1.3-1.5x the weaker measured tail, with margin for run-to-run variance; per-core floor below the weaker measured factor.

per_core_scaling.comment: achieved_N_core_throughput / (single_core_throughput * N) must be >= linear_tolerance_min. Weaker measured factor e2 0.703 (c3 0.671); floor 0.6 with margin.

measurements.note: v2 canonical envelope (RFC 9421 carrier, concurrency 128 / 8000 requests), GKE 2026-07-14 (current-source re-measurement on the delegated-required-only path, KMS-rooted via Workload Identity). throughput=verified responses/sec; latency microseconds; scaling = tput_8/(tput_1*8). Tracks the 2026-07-12 declaration within run-to-run variance (e2 402.1->395.6, c3 499.4->492.9); floors unchanged.

production_slo.envelope_note: v2 canonical envelope: RFC 9421 carrier, concurrency 128 / 8000 requests, cold TLS1.3-mTLS (keepalive_reuse=0). These are cold-connection ENVELOPE figures for regression detection on declared hardware, not user-facing steady-state latency (production reuses connections). Tails are fatter than the superseded v1 (64/2000) run because concurrency doubled and requests 4x'd.

absolute_gates.kind: hardware-independent correctness gates — always required, every release

gates.name: replay-race
gates.requirement: N concurrent submissions of the same signed request across cores and across replicas yield EXACTLY ONE Fresh and N-1 Replay.

gates.name: bounded-drain
gates.requirement: Graceful drain joins all in-flight requests within the bounded grace window; zero abandoned requests.
